openmeter/dedupe/memorydedupe.purpose: In-memory LRU-backed deduplication for CloudEvents, used as the no-dependency fallback when Redis is unavailable. Implements the dedupe.Deduplicator interface using a fixed-size hashicorp/golang-lru cache keyed by dedupe.Item.Key().

patterns.name: LRU-cache keyed by dedupe.Item.Key()
patterns.description: All dedup checks use item.Key() (a composite of Namespace+ID+Source) as the LRU map key with nil values; never store event payloads.

patterns.name: Implement all dedupe.Deduplicator methods
patterns.description: Must implement IsUnique, CheckUnique, Set, Close, and CheckUniqueBatch to satisfy the dedupe.Deduplicator interface.

patterns.name: CheckUniqueBatch returns split result sets
patterns.description: Always initialise both UniqueItems and AlreadyProcessedItems in CheckUniqueBatchResult with pre-allocated ItemSet maps before ranging over items.
patterns.example: result := dedupe.CheckUniqueBatchResult{UniqueItems: make(dedupe.ItemSet, len(items)), AlreadyProcessedItems: make(dedupe.ItemSet, len(items))}

key_file_guides.role: Single-file implementation of the in-memory Deduplicator; NewDeduplicator(size) is the only constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: defaultSize=1024 is the fallback when size<1; callers must choose a meaningful size to avoid premature eviction causing false-unique results.

key_file_guides.role: Validates IsUnique atomically adds to the cache (second call returns false) and that CheckUnique+Set are separate read/write operations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use context.Background() — acceptable in tests but not in production code.

anti_patterns: Storing event payload data in the LRU cache (only nil values, keyed by item.Key())
Skipping Close() implementation — even if it's a no-op it must exist to satisfy the interface
Using context.Background() in production implementation code (only in tests)

decisions.rationale: Bounded memory footprint for long-running sink workers; evicted entries can cause duplicate events to slip through, which is acceptable for the in-memory fallback.
A single LRU call avoids a check-then-set race under concurrent ingest goroutines.

code_examples.scenario: Implement a new Deduplicator backed by a different store

openmeter/dedupe/redisdedupe.purpose: Redis-backed deduplication for CloudEvents in the sink worker. Supports three key-format modes (rawkey, keyhash, keyhash-migration) and exposes the same dedupe.Deduplicator interface as memorydedupe, but with TTL-based expiry and pipeline batch operations.

patterns.name: Mode-switch on every key operation
patterns.description: Every method (IsUnique, CheckUnique, Set, CheckUniqueBatch) switches on d.Mode to determine the Redis key format; never assume a single format.

patterns.name: SetArgs with NX + TTL for atomic set-if-not-exists
patterns.description: Use redis.SetArgs{TTL: d.Expiration, Mode: "nx"} for all uniqueness writes; interpret redis.Nil return as duplicate (key already existed).
patterns.example: status, err := d.Redis.SetArgs(ctx, key, "", redis.SetArgs{TTL: d.Expiration, Mode: "nx"}).Result()

patterns.name: Pipelined batch writes
patterns.description: Set() pipelines multiple SetArgs calls; inspect per-command errors (redis.Nil = already existed) to build the returned existingItems slice.
patterns.example: cmds, err := d.Redis.Pipelined(ctx, func(pipe redis.Pipeliner) error { ... })

patterns.name: xxh3 128-bit hash → base64url key
patterns.description: GetKeyHash() uses xxh3.HashString128 + base64.RawURLEncoding to shrink keys by ~57% in Redis memory versus the raw composite key.

key_file_guides.role: Full Redis Deduplicator implementation; DedupeMode type and its three constants are the API extension point for key-format migration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: keyhash-migration mode checks both old rawkey and new keyhash Redis keys to prevent false-unique events during rolling deploy; do not simplify without coordinating a cutover.

key_file_guides.role: Standalone hashing helper; must not be changed without re-hashing all existing Redis keys.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Changing the hash algorithm or encoding invalidates all keys currently in Redis and will cause a flood of duplicate events.

anti_patterns: Calling Redis without checking d.Redis != nil (IsUnique already guards this, replicate the guard in new methods)
Using SET without NX — allows overwriting existing keys and defeats deduplication
Adding a new DedupeMode without updating every method's switch statement
Ignoring redis.Nil in pipeline results — it signals a pre-existing key, not an error
Changing GetKeyHash algorithm without a migration mode and Redis key rotation plan

decisions.decision: Three-mode key format with explicit migration mode
decisions.rationale: Allows a rolling upgrade from rawkey to keyhash without a big-bang cutover; migration mode checks both formats so no events are incorrectly classified as unique during the transition window.

decisions.decision: xxh3-128 + base64url over SHA-224 or raw composite strings
decisions.rationale: 22-char base64url keys use 57% less Redis memory than the ~77-char raw composite key while maintaining collision probability ~1e-30 at 300M events/day.

decisions.decision: TTL-based expiry instead of unbounded growth
decisions.rationale: Ingest events are only deduplicated within a recent window; expired keys free memory automatically without a separate purge job.
